item.text: The conflict is exactly the merge log described: the shell from ST2/ST3 and the matrix workbench from ST4 both rewrote the same app surfaces. I’m mapping the current file layout now so I can merge against the actual modules instead of guessing names.

item.text: I’ve confirmed the app shell already contains ST2/ST3 functionality, and ST4 added a separate `MatrixWorkbench` plus matrix CSS classes into the shared stylesheet. Next I’m checking the test expectations so the merge resolution preserves the intended artifact surface and not just syntax.

item.aggregated_output:
from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path


REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]


def test_matrix_modules_and_ui_exist() -> None:
    engine = REPO_ROOT / "src" / "core" / "matrix" / "engine.ts"
    service = REPO_ROOT / "src" / "services" / "matrix" / "service.ts"
    workbench = REPO_ROOT / "src" / "features" / "matrix" / "MatrixWorkbench.tsx"
    app = REPO_ROOT / "src" / "app" / "App.tsx"

    assert engine.exists()
    assert service.exists()
    assert workbench.exists()
    assert "MatrixWorkbench" in workbench.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "MatrixWorkbench" in app.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

item.text: The `App.tsx` conflict left a half-merged branch that references non-existent `settingsDocument` and `workspaceDocument` values, so this is more than marker cleanup. I’m folding ST4’s `MatrixWorkbench` into the existing ST2/ST3 app state and keeping the shell-owned persistence, history, and memory behavior intact.

item.text: I have the merge shape pinned down: `App.tsx` needs a real semantic merge, not a side pick. I’m editing it to keep the persisted shell/state management while mounting `MatrixWorkbench` for the matrix tab, then I’ll merge the shared and matrix-specific CSS blocks.

item.aggregated_output:
# Research Notes

- 2026-03-27: Scaffolded the calculator baseline with a Vite + React + TypeScript frontend, Tauri Rust shell, versioned persistence schemas, and shared service contracts under `src/core/contracts` to lock computation boundaries before feature nodes land.
- 2026-03-27: Verification succeeded with `python -m pytest`, `npm run build`, and `cargo check --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml`. A minimal Windows `.ico` asset was added under `src-tauri/icons` so Tauri can compile on Windows without pulling broader branding work into this node.
- 2026-03-27: Recovered the merged ST2/ST3/ST4 batch by resolving the `src/app/App.tsx` cherry-pick conflict in favor of the persisted shell, embedding ST3's live expression runtime into the existing calculate tab, namespacing `src/features/calculate/calculate.css` to avoid global UI collisions, and re-verifying with `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test:unit`, and `python -m pytest`.

item.text:
Resolved the ST2/ST3/ST4 cherry-pick by merging the persisted shell in [App.tsx](C:/Users/alber/OneDrive/문서/GitHub/calculator/src/app/App.tsx) with ST4’s `MatrixWorkbench`, and by folding the matrix UI styles into the shared [app.css](C:/Users/alber/OneDrive/문서/GitHub/calculator/src/app/app.css). The result keeps the existing settings/history/memory shell intact, mounts the matrix workflow on the matrix tab, and removes the broken half-merged references that were blocking the batch.

I also recorded the recovery in [RESEARCH_NOTES.md](C:/Users/alber/OneDrive/문서/GitHub/codex_auto/.codex-auto-workspace/projects/c-users-alber-onedrive-github-calculator-main-b2c8e74450/docs/RESEARCH_NOTES.md). The cherry-pick is complete, and `git status` is clean aside from the branch being ahead.

Verification passed:
- `npm run typecheck`
- `npm run test:unit`
- `python -m pytest`

items.text: Inspect conflicted app files and dependent modules to identify the exact merge breakage
Resolve App.tsx and app.css conflicts with the smallest coherent merged implementation
Run verification and add concise recovery notes if the repair is material
